name: Sajazarra Castle and Vineyards Loop
name_original: Cihuri, Sajazarra, Galbárruli – circular por Anguciana
description: Explore the moderate 28.0 km Cihuri, Sajazarra, Galbárruli circular mountain biking route via Anguciana, featuring historic villages and sce
region_intro: Mountain biking around Cihuri offers diverse terrain within the Rioja Alta area of Spain. The region is characterized by extensive vineyards, riverine landscapes formed by the Tirón and Oja rivers, and a mountainous backdrop provided by the Peñas de Jembres and Montes Obarenes. These features contribute to varied routes, from paths through agricultural areas to more challenging trails in the foothills. The Oja-Tirón Ruta Verde also provides dedicated cycling paths. Best mountain bike trails around Cihuri The most popular mountain bike trail is Sajazarra Castle and Vineyards Loop, a 17.4 miles (28.0 km) trail that takes 2 hours 36 minutes to complete. This moderate route winds through vineyards and past historical landmarks. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Sajazarra Castle – Monasterio de Herrera loop from Cuzcurrita de Río Tirón, a difficult 39.6 miles (63.8 km) path. This longer route features significant elevation changes and panoramic views of the surrounding landscape. Local mountain bikers also love the La Rioja Viewpoint – Monasterio de Herrera loop from Cuzcurrita de Río Tirón, a 30.2 miles (48.6 km) trail leading through varied terrain with notable viewpoints, often completed in about 4 hours 47 minutes. Mountain biking around Cihuri is defined by rolling vineyards, river valleys, and the protective mountain ranges of the Peñas de Jembres and Montes Obarenes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy routes to more strenuous climbs. The routes in Cihuri are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 90 reviews. More than 600 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Cihuri's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Cihuri?
A: Cihuri offers a wide selection of mountain bike trails, with over 100 routes available. These range from easy paths through vineyards to more challenging climbs in the foothills of the surrounding mountains.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners or families in Cihuri?
A: Yes, Cihuri has a good selection of easier routes. There are 21 easy trails, many of which follow the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20511376" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Oja-Tirón Ruta Verde&lt;/a&gt; or wind through the flatter vineyard terrain, making them suitable for beginners or family outings. These routes often feature less elevation gain and smoother surfaces.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Cihuri?
A: The terrain around Cihuri is quite varied. You'll find routes that traverse extensive vineyards, offering picturesque views and relatively flat sections. Other trails follow the lush riverbanks of the Tirón and Oja rivers. For more challenging rides, trails ascend into the foothills of the Peñas de Jembres and Montes Obarenes, providing significant elevation changes and panoramic vistas.

Q: Are there any long-distance or challenging mountain bike routes in Cihuri?
A: Absolutely. For experienced riders seeking a challenge, Cihuri offers 17 difficult trails. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38192258" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sajazarra Castle – Monasterio de Herrera loop from Cuzcurrita de Río Tirón&lt;/a&gt; cover over 60 km with substantial elevation gain, providing a demanding and rewarding experience.

Q: What historical landmarks or attractions can I see while mountain biking in Cihuri?
A: Cihuri's trails often pass by significant historical sites. You can cycle near the ancient Roman Bridge, explore the unique Barrio de las Bodegas (winery neighborhood), or incorporate a visit to the magnificent &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5343958" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sajazarra Castle&lt;/a&gt;. Other nearby castles include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4705240" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de los Velasco&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5338536" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Velasco Castle and Cuzcurrita de Río Tirón Bridge&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any mountain viewpoints or scenic spots along the trails?
A: Yes, many trails offer stunning views. The region is known for its rolling vineyards and river valleys. For elevated perspectives, consider routes that lead to viewpoints like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4199700" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cruz de Motrico&lt;/a&gt; or summits such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3406983" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña la Abeja&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4035773" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñalrayo&lt;/a&gt;, which provide panoramic vistas of the Rioja Alta landscape.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Cihuri?
A: The mountain bike trails in Cihuri are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.6 out of 5 stars from over 90 reviews. Riders frequently praise the diverse scenery, from the extensive vineyards to the riverine landscapes, and the variety of routes catering to different skill levels.

Q: Are there any loop trails for mountain biking in Cihuri?
A: Yes, Cihuri is well-suited for loop rides. Many popular mountain biking routes are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993872149/sajazarra-castle-and-vineyards-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sajazarra Castle and Vineyards Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20511376" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente de Cihuri – Haro loop from Castañares de Rioja&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Cihuri?
A: Cihuri benefits from a favorable microclimate due to its location protected by mountain ranges. Spring and autumn are generally ideal, offering pleasant temperatures and vibrant landscapes, especially during harvest season in the vineyards. While winters are mild, checking local conditions is always advisable.

Q: Are there any routes that combine mountain biking with wine tasting opportunities?
A: Given Cihuri's location in the heart of Rioja Alta, many mountain bike trails weave through extensive vineyards. This provides excellent opportunities to combine your ride with visits to local wineries or the traditional Barrio de las Bodegas, where you can experience the region's renowned wine culture.

Q: What are some less-known or 'hidden gem' mountain bike trails in Cihuri?
A: While specific 'hidden gems' are often discovered by local exploration, komoot identifies Cihuri as a region with 20 hidden gems for outdoor activities. These often include quieter paths through lesser-traveled vineyard sections or routes that lead to secluded river spots, offering a more tranquil riding experience away from the main routes.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails that follow rivers or greenways in Cihuri?
A: Yes, Cihuri is situated near the confluence of the Oja and Tirón rivers, and an extension of the 'Vía Verde' system, known as the Oja-Tirón Ruta Verde, passes through the village. This provides dedicated paths along the riverbanks, offering tranquil rides through lush fluvial vegetation.
tips:
In the town of Sajazarra there is a magnificent walled castle.

Between the 12th and 13th centuries the town was fortified. In its beginnings there was a single tower that was destroyed to build, during the second half of the fifteenth century, the current castle.

In the center of the fortress is the keep and, although it is not currently visible, it is believed that there was also a defensive moat.

Following the winemaking tradition of the area, today this beautiful complex made up of the castle, the fortification, the Roman bridge and its gardens, is a winery.
Galbárruli is a municipality belonging to the Haro region, located at an altitude of 652 m, on the southern slope of the Obaranes mountains. The place is practically deserted.
One of the most beautiful viewpoints in this area. It is to stay a while to enjoy the views.

The monastery of Nuestra Señora de la Piedad, better known simply as the monastery of La Piedad, in Casalarreina, La Rioja, is a monastery of contemplative Dominican nuns. It is a spectacular place, one of the most important but least known in La Rioja. The monastery and all the history it collects is impressive. With a guided tour you learn a lot and the price is very affordable.

faqs.question: What level of mountain biking skill is required for this route?
faqs.answer: This route is rated as moderate and corresponds to approximately STS S2 on the Single Track Scale. This means you should expect terrain with roots and stones, gradients up to 70%, and potentially tight curves. It requires good physical fitness and advanced riding skills.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this mountain biking route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied, typical for a moderate mountain bike trail. You'll encounter sections with roots and stones, steep gradients up to 70%, and tight curves. There's also a short 'off-grid section' which suggests less maintained, more natural areas.

faqs.question: Is this a loop trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, the 'Cihuri, Sajazarra, Galbárruli – circular route via Anguciana' is a circular route, meaning it starts and ends in the same general area.

faqs.question: What interesting sights will I encounter on this circular route?
faqs.answer: You'll pass through several historical and scenic points, including the magnificent walled Sajazarra Castle, the nearly uninhabited Galbárruli Village, and the Monastery of Our Lady of Mercy in Casalarreina.

faqs.question: Are there any notable viewpoints along the trail?
faqs.answer: Absolutely! A highlight of the ride is the La Rioja Viewpoint (Aussichtspunkt La Rioja), which is described as one of the most beautiful viewpoints in the area, offering expansive views.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other official trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of several official trails, including the Ruta Verde del Tirón, Sendero del vino A-4, Sendero del vino A-3, and the Vía Verde del Río Oja, among others.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to ride this mountain biking route?
faqs.answer: While specific recommendations aren't provided, mountain biking in the La Rioja region is generally pleasant during spring and autumn when temperatures are milder. Summer can be hot, and winter might bring colder, wetter conditions.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Cihuri, Sajazarra, Galbárruli mountain biking route?
faqs.answer: The route is a circular one, starting and ending in the Cihuri area. While specific parking locations aren't detailed, you would typically look for parking in or near Cihuri, Anguciana, or Sajazarra to begin your ride.

faqs.question: Is the Cihuri, Sajazarra, Galbárruli mountain biking route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: The provided information does not specify whether dogs are allowed on this particular mountain biking route. It's always best to check local regulations or signage at the trailhead regarding pets.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to ride this trail?
faqs.answer: There is no information indicating that a permit or entrance fee is required to ride the 'Cihuri, Sajazarra, Galbárruli – circular route via Anguciana'. Most public trails in Spain do not require permits for recreational use.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or stay near the route?
faqs.answer: The route passes through several villages like Cihuri, Sajazarra, Galbárruli, and Anguciana. You can expect to find cafes, pubs, restaurants, and accommodation options in these settlements, especially in the larger towns like Sajazarra and Anguciana.
